mechanism: Broad-spectrum kinase inhibitor with activity against FLT3 and other kinases
additional_notes: Originally developed as BCR-ABL inhibitor; shows preclinical activity in FLT3-ITD AML.

mechanism: Inhibits DOT1L histone methyltransferase, targeting MLL-rearranged leukemias
additional_notes: Also known as EPZ-5676; rationale strongest in MLL-rearranged AML (e.g., MOLM-13).

mechanism: Selective MCL-1 inhibitor that induces apoptosis in MCL-1 dependent cells
additional_notes: Investigational compound; targets MCL-1-mediated venetoclax resistance in AML.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, promoting differentiation and apoptosis
additional_notes: Investigated in AML trials and combinations; typically micromolar activity in AML lines.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increases oxidative stress and perturbs proteostasis
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate identified in AML screens; often reported as low micromolar cytotoxic.

mechanism: Pleiotropic modulator (Wnt/STAT/mTOR and other pathways) with reported anticancer effects
additional_notes: Identified in phenotypic screens for anticancer activity; potency varies by cell line.

mechanism: Multiple effects including proteasome inhibition and aldehyde dehydrogenase modulation; activity often enhanced with copper
additional_notes: Preclinical AML cytotoxicity reported; activity can be condition-dependent (e.g., copper co-treatment).

mechanism: Modulates Hedgehog and mTOR signaling and other pleiotropic effects
additional_notes: Reported anticancer activity in some hematologic models and drug-repurposing screens.
Repurposing reports show activity across various cancer models including hematologic lines.

mechanism: Inhibits the mevalonate pathway, impacting lipid biosynthesis and prenylation processes
additional_notes: Statins can impair AML growth and sensitize cells to other agents; often require higher micromolar concentrations in vitro.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism via AMPK activation and mTOR pathway modulation
additional_notes: Reported AML growth inhibitory effects often observed at higher (micromolar to millimolar) in vitro concentrations.

mechanism: Reported lysosomal/mitochondrial perturbation leading to cytotoxicity in cancer screens
additional_notes: Identified in drug screens for AML cytotoxicity; mechanism in cancer context is pleiotropic.

mechanism: Immunomodulatory and other anticancer activities reported in preclinical studies
additional_notes: Reported in phenotypic screens and preclinical hematologic models as a repurposing candidate.
